records.ety: [OE. until, ontil; un- (as in unto) + til till; cf. Dan. indtil, Sw. intill. See Unto, and Till, prep.]

defs: To; unto; towards; -- used of material objects.
quotes: Taverners until them told the same.
He roused himself full blithe, and hastened them until.

defs: To; up to; till; before; -- used of time; as, he staid until evening; he will not come back until the end of the month.
quotes: He and his sons were priests to the tribe of Dan until the day of the captivity.
notes: ☞ In contracts and like documents until is construed as exclusive of the date mentioned unless it was the manifest intent of the parties to include it.

defs: As far as; to the place or degree that; especially, up to the time that; till. See Till, conj.
quotes: In open prospect nothing bounds our eye, Until the earth seems joined unto the sky.
But the rest of the dead lives not again until the thousand years were finished.
